headline: **MLB Daily Roundup: April 24, 2026**
article:
The Atlanta Braves secured a 7-2 victory over the Washington Nationals, led by Ozzie Albies' impressive performance at the plate, where he hit 3 home runs and drove in 4 runs. The Detroit Tigers edged out the Milwaukee Brewers 5-4 in a closely contested matchup, with Riley Greene leading the Tigers' offense with a 2-hit, 1-home run performance and 2 RBIs. The Chicago Cubs edged out the Philadelphia Phillies 7-8 in a thrilling matchup, with Brandon Marsh leading the Phillies' offense with 3 hits, including 2 home runs, and 3 RBIs.

Several key players have been impacted by injuries, including Edwin Uceta, who has been placed on the 60-day injured list due to a right shoulder impingement with the Tampa Bay Rays. Other injured players include Ryan Feltner (Colorado Rockies), Dean Kremer (Baltimore Orioles), Robert Garcia (Texas Rangers), Francisco Lindor (New York Mets), Dylan Dodd (Atlanta Braves), Bryan Hoeing (San Diego Padres), and Wyatt Langford (Texas Rangers).

The Toronto Blue Jays have acquired catcher Willie MacIver in a trade with the Texas Rangers, who received cash in return. The Seattle Mariners traded RHP Casey Legumina to the Tampa Bay Rays for RHP Ty Cummings. The Colorado Rockies traded 3B Nicky Lopez to the Chicago Cubs for cash, while the Houston Astros traded RHP Wilmy Sanchez to the New York Yankees for SS Braden Shewmake.

top_news.title: **Braves Take Down Nationals 7-2**
top_news.summary: The Atlanta Braves secured a 7-2 victory over the Washington Nationals, led by Ozzie Albies' impressive performance at the plate. Albies hit 3 home runs, including a 5th homer of the season, and drove in 4 runs.

top_news.title: **Tigers Take Down Brewers in Thrilling Finish**
top_news.summary: The Detroit Tigers edged out the Milwaukee Brewers 5-4 in a closely contested matchup. Riley Greene led the Tigers' offense with a 2-hit, 1-home run performance, driving in 2 runs.

top_news.title: **Cubs Comeback Victory: Phillies Fall 7-8**
top_news.summary: The Chicago Cubs edged out the Philadelphia Phillies 7-8 in a thrilling matchup. Brandon Marsh led the Phillies' offense with 3 hits, including 2 home runs, and 3 RBIs.

top_news.title: **Padres Take Down Rockies in Thrilling 10-8 Victory**
top_news.summary: The San Diego Padres secured a hard-fought win over the Colorado Rockies, outscoring them 10-8. In a standout performance, Mickey Moniak led the Padres' offense with 4 hits, 2 home runs, and 2 RBIs.

top_news.title: **Rays RHP Edwin Uceta Lands on 60-Day Injured List**
top_news.summary: Tampa Bay Rays right-hander Edwin Uceta has been placed on the 60-day injured list due to a right shoulder impingement. The injury update marks a status change for the team.

top_news.title: **Blue Jays Acquire Willie MacIver in Trade**
top_news.summary: The Toronto Blue Jays have acquired catcher Willie MacIver in a trade with the Texas Rangers, who received cash in return.

New York Mets placed SS Francisco Lindor on the 10-day injured list. Left calf strain. | Status Change
Atlanta Braves placed LHP Dylan Dodd on the 15-day injured list retroactive to April 22, 2026. Left thoracic spine inflammation. | Status Change
San Diego Padres transferred RHP Bryan Hoeing from the 15-day injured list to the 60-day injured list. Torn right elbow flexor tendon. | Status Change
Texas Rangers placed LF Wyatt Langford on the 10-day injured list. Right forearm strain. | Status Change
Philadelphia Phillies transferred RHP Max Lazar from the 15-day injured list to the 60-day injured list. Left oblique strain. | Status Change
Philadelphia Phillies placed C J.T. Realmuto on the 10-day injured list. Back spasms. | Status Change
Detroit Tigers placed LHP Bailey Horn on the 60-day injured list. Left elbow arthroscopy recovery. | Status Change
Boston Red Sox transferred 1B Triston Casas from the 10-day injured list to the 60-day injured list. Recovery from left patellar tendon repair. | Status Change
